code:
proof fn phi_1_remove_proc_owned_threads_unconstrained(
    root_proc: ProcPtr,
    proc_tree_dom: Set<ProcPtr>,
    old_proc_perms: Map<ProcPtr, PointsTo<Process>>,
    new_proc_perms: Map<ProcPtr, PointsTo<Process>>,
    proc_ptr: ProcPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        remove_proc_ensures(root_proc, proc_tree_dom, old_proc_perms, new_proc_perms, proc_ptr),
        old_proc_perms[proc_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        !new_proc_perms.dom().contains(proc_ptr) || !new_proc_perms[proc_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
{
}
reason: `remove_proc_ensures` removes the proc from the tree domain but never constrains cleanup of its `owned_threads` Ghost field — threads owned by the removed process could remain dangling

name: remove_proc_subtree_set_not_cleaned
code:
proof fn phi_2_remove_proc_subtree_set_not_cleaned(
    root_proc: ProcPtr,
    proc_tree_dom: Set<ProcPtr>,
    old_proc_perms: Map<ProcPtr, PointsTo<Process>>,
    new_proc_perms: Map<ProcPtr, PointsTo<Process>>,
    proc_ptr: ProcPtr,
    parent_ptr: ProcPtr,
)
    requires
        remove_proc_ensures(root_proc, proc_tree_dom, old_proc_perms, new_proc_perms, proc_ptr),
        old_proc_perms[proc_ptr].value().parent == Some(parent_ptr),
        proc_tree_dom.contains(parent_ptr),
    ensures
        !new_proc_perms[parent_ptr].value().subtree_set@.contains(proc_ptr),
{
}
reason: When a proc is removed, its parent's `subtree_set` should no longer contain the removed proc — if `remove_proc_ensures` doesn't constrain parent subtree cleanup, stale references persist

name: remove_proc_sibling_uppertree_unchanged
code:
proof fn phi_4_remove_proc_sibling_uppertree_unchanged(
    root_proc: ProcPtr,
    proc_tree_dom: Set<ProcPtr>,
    old_proc_perms: Map<ProcPtr, PointsTo<Process>>,
    new_proc_perms: Map<ProcPtr, PointsTo<Process>>,
    proc_ptr: ProcPtr,
    sibling: ProcPtr,
)
    requires
        remove_proc_ensures(root_proc, proc_tree_dom, old_proc_perms, new_proc_perms, proc_ptr),
        proc_tree_dom.contains(sibling),
        sibling != proc_ptr,
    ensures
        new_proc_perms[sibling].value().uppertree_seq@ == old_proc_perms[sibling].value().uppertree_seq@,
{
}
reason: `remove_proc_ensures` should preserve uppertree_seq for all non-removed procs — if it doesn't constrain this, the tree ancestry path could silently change for siblings

proof fn phi_5_sll_len_without_wf_returns_value_list_len(sll: StaticLinkedList<ProcPtr, CONTAINER_PROC_LIST_LEN>)
    requires

    ensures

reason: The external_body `len()` unconditionally ensures `l == self.value_list_len` regardless of `wf()` — a malformed list reports its cached length field which may disagree with actual spec_seq length, enabling inconsistent reasoning
